2604.25902.title: Toward a Functional Geometric Algebra for Natural Language Semantics
2604.25902.paper_summary: Distributional and neural approaches to natural language semantics have been built almost exclusively on conventional linear algebra: vectors, matrices, tensors, and the operations that accompany them. These methods have achieved remarkable empirical success, yet they face persistent structural limitations in compositional semantics, type sensitivity, and interpretability. I argue in this paper that geometric algebra (GA) -- specifically, Clifford algebras -- provides a mathematically superior foundation for semantic representation, and that a Functional Geometric Algebra (FGA) framework extends GA toward a typed, compositional semantics capable of supporting inference, transformation, and interpretability while retaining full compatibility with distributional learning and modern neural architectures. I develop the formal foundations, identify three core capabilities that GA provides and linear algebra does not, present a detailed worked example illustrating operator-level semantic contrasts, and show how GA-based operations already implicit in current transformer architectures can be made explicit and extended. The central claim is not merely increased dimensionality but increased structural organization: GA expands an $n$-dimensional embedding space into a $2^n$ multivector algebra where base semantic concepts and their higher-order interactions are represented within a single, principled algebraic framework.
2604.25902.paper_summary_zh: 分佈式和神經網絡方法在自然語言語義學上幾乎完全建立在傳統的線性代數之上：向量、矩陣、張量及其伴隨的運算。這些方法取得了顯著的實證成功，但在組合語義學、類型敏感性和可解釋性方面面臨持續的結構限制。本文中我主張，幾何代數（GA）——特別是克利福德代數——為語義表示提供了一個數學上更優越的基礎，而功能性幾何代數（FGA）框架則將GA擴展到一個類型化的組合語義，能夠支持推理、轉換和可解釋性，同時保持與分佈式學習和現代神經架構的完全兼容。我發展了形式基礎，確定了GA提供而線性代數不具備的三個核心能力，展示了一個詳細的工作示例，說明了運算子級別的語義對比，並展示了如何將當前Transformer架構中已隱含的基於GA的運算明確化並擴展。中心主張不僅僅是增加維度，而是增加結構組織：GA將一個$n$維嵌入空間擴展為一個$2^n$多向量代數，其中基本語義概念及其高階互動在一個單一的、原則性的代數框架內表示。
2604.25902.author: James Pustejovsky et.al.
